mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with declining housing starts signals a rotation from cyclical construction to manufacturing and technology sectors, which historically outperform during inventory accumulation phases and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.05, or SPX returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises and HOUST falls simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs above 3.0 percent, each basis point increase in the federal funds rate disproportionately compresses equity valuations by raising the real discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive or neutral in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the coefficient on FEDFUNDS becomes statistically insignificant when CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in consumer credit outstanding reflects expanding household purchasing power and economic confidence, which directly supports corporate sales growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month changes, or the signal produces losses in more than 55% of rising credit episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index breaks above its 12-month moving average, it confirms a supply-side recovery that alleviates bottlenecks and improves corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX fails to outperform its baseline by at least 1.5% over 60 days when INDPRO crosses above its trailing average, or the signal generates negative returns in more than 60% of breakout events.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the unemployment rate and growth in real GDP indicate a self-reinforcing expansion where hiring accelerates consumer spending, which historically precedes equity bull markets and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04, or SPX returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when UNRATE falls and GDPC1 rises concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid housing market cooling where housing starts fall below 1.0 million units annually, construction-related corporate earnings deteriorate and consumer wealth effects reverse, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of instances where HOUST drops below 1000, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.
